description_long: This airplane features a futuristic, angular design with a sleek, streamlined fuselage and sharply contoured wings. The wings are swept back and appear to have multiple layers or fins, giving the aircraft an aggressive, high-performance look. The body is predominantly metallic gray with a small, distinct green cockpit canopy situated near the front. The underside and side views reveal additional aerodynamic surfaces and possible weapon or sensor pods integrated into the structure. The tail section is split, with twin vertical stabilizers and additional horizontal surfaces, contributing to its advanced appearance. The overall form suggests a high-speed, maneuverable craft, possibly inspired by modern fighter jets or science fiction vehicles.
description: A sleek, angular airplane with swept-back, multi-layered wings, a green cockpit canopy, and twin vertical stabilizers, finished in metallic gray tones.
description_view_0: Front view shows the pointed nose, green cockpit canopy, and the layered, swept-back wings extending outward.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the elongated fuselage, swept wings, and the complex undercarriage or pod structures beneath the wings.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the twin vertical stabilizers, horizontal tail surfaces, and the layered wing structure from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the aerodynamic profile, wing layers, and tail configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with swept, multi-layered wings and pronounced anhedral angles; no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines integrated into the rear fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Twin vertical stabilizers with a split tail and additional horizontal stabilizers, resembling an H-tail configuration.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear, with main gear under the wings and a nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a distinct green canopy integrated into the fuselage.
